output.summary_final: {"title": "Building Trails That Last: Skills, Maintenance, and Decision Making", "bullets": ["Prioritize braking earlier and smoother to generate speed rather than taking unnecessary risks.", "Maintain focus on the trail exit on berms by keeping hips centered and building pressure gradually.", "Ensure the bike feels quiet and predictable to keep attention on the trail instead of mechanical noises.", "Adopt a short, boring maintenance routine that includes checking bolts, measuring chain wear, and cleaning stanchions.", "Select appropriate tire pressure and casing choices, as these often matter more than minor suspension tweaks.", "Solve mystery creaks by cleaning contact surfaces, re-greasing threads, and re-torquing components to spec.", "Manage wet conditions through decision-making skills that know when to back off to protect traction and trails.", "Improve faster by repeating one trail segment and changing only one variable at a time during practice."]}

{"title": "On-Call That Doesn't Break People", "bullets": ["Design on-call rotations so waking up is rare and responses are obvious when needed.", "Implement secrets handling with short-lived, narrowly scoped tokens rotated via automation.", "Treat system failures as expected events and contain them with timeouts, retries, and clear ownership.", "Conduct lightweight threat modeling by identifying assets, entry points, trust boundaries, and abuse cases.", "Write down assumptions to turn disagreement into collaboration rather than personal conflict.", "Use RFCs that start with context and constraints, list options, and state explicit risks.", "Make alerting action-oriented; avoid paging if no one can take immediate remediation steps.", "Build error budgets that change behavior rather than serving as unused charts during planning."]}

{"title": "Wreck Diving Fundamentals: Calm, Navigation, and Exit Strategies", "bullets": ["The calmest diver is often the safest because calm breathing keeps decisions deliberate and reduces panic.", "For navigation, select one primary reference like heading plus depth and one backup like reef contour plus current direction.", "A backup light is not just redundancy; it changes your mental state by providing options when things go wrong.", "Most underwater stress comes from surprise and lack of rehearsal, not from the difficulty of the problem itself.", "Good buoyancy control protects the environment and reduces task load for navigation and photography during the dive.", "A wreck can feel like a hallway, but silt can turn it into a blank room in seconds if you fin hard.", "The most valuable skill is the ability to call a dive early without ego to ensure a safe exit.", "Neutral buoyancy drills like hovering and slow fin pivots make everything else easier when conditions get choppy."]}

{"title": "Mastering Underwater Photography: Lighting, Workflow, and Habits", "bullets": ["Great underwater images stem from a clear concept and a simple setup executed consistently rather than complex gear.", "Adjusting the strobe angle away from the lens axis is a critical technique to dramatically reduce backscatter.", "Lighting is fundamentally about control, managing where shadows fall and preventing unwanted spill onto the background.", "In mixed lighting scenarios, identify the dominant color temperature first and correct all other elements relative to that decision.", "A grid softener is primarily used to control light spill and keep dark areas dark, not just to create softness.", "Documentary work relies on strict routines including backups and notes to prevent catastrophic data loss.", "Labeling files and writing captions immediately after a shoot prevents memory gaps that can ruin long-term projects.", "One-light practice is more educational than a complicated kit because it forces the photographer to learn proper placement."]}

{"title": "Index Investing Without the Myths", "bullets": ["Risk is defined as the chance of not meeting goals or suffering permanent loss, which is distinct from volatility.", "Index investing succeeds by reducing costs and decision errors rather than predicting future market movements.", "Lowering fees acts like increasing returns without requiring the investor to take on additional risk.", "Position sizing rules are essential to prevent a single great idea from becoming a portfolio-ending mistake.", "The hardest part of investing is behavior, specifically staying consistent when headlines are loud and portfolios are down.", "Diversification requires different underlying drivers, not just different tickers that may move together.", "A 50% loss requires a 100% gain to recover, meaning drawdowns are both a mathematical and emotional challenge.", "Good frameworks are boring and rely on diversification, liquidity, and rules that survive your worst moods."]}
